text: In this video we present the case of sixty nine years old man, who developed a left ureteral stricture in presence of a nine millimeters stone. Preliminary ureteroscopy confirmed a tight ureteral stricture. A nephrostomy tub was placed and a combined pyelography showed a length of about three centimeters of the stricture.
A robot assisted ureteroplasty with buccal mucosa graft was planned.

The surgeon performs flexible ureteroscopy, inserting a guide wire through the ureteral stricture. Under translumination and Firefly tool guidance, the ureter is prepared, with precise characterization of the stricture's length, limits, and tissue quality. The lower limit is determined using translumination, while the upper limit is assessed with transnephrostomic ICG injection. Intravenous ICG injection provides visual assessment of tissue quality.

The surgeon incises the ureteral wall while preserving the posterior plate, removes the stone, and harvests a buccal mucosa graft from the cheek. The graft is sutured with four half sutures to prevent curling, and a ureteral stent is placed using an endoscope.
The surgeon opens Girota's fascia, using transillumination by ureteroscope to locate and guide the dissection of the left ureter.

The surgeon uses transnephrostomic ICG injection and endoscopic transillumination to identify the proximal and distal ends of the ureteral stricture. The proximal end is incised, revealing the guide wire. The distal end is also incised, followed by a longitudinal incision along the anterior wall of the stricture, while preserving the posterior plate.
The surgeon progressively removes the stone impacted in the ureteral mucosa. A caliper is used to measure the length of the ureteral stricture.

The surgeon performs a complete flexible ureteroscopy to search for residual stones. The buccal mucosa graft, previously harvested from the patient's cheek, is sutured onto the ureter using a four-zero absorbable suture in a running fashion, with four half sutures to prevent curling of the graft.
An omentum flap is created and wrapped around the buccal mucosa patch to support its vascularization. Teseal is applied to protect the patch and the surrounding area.

Operative time was two hundred thirty minutes, console time one hundred fifty minutes, no perioperative complications were reported. The bladder catheter was removed after four days, the abdominal drain was removed after five days, nephrostomy tube was closed after ten days, ureteral stent was removed after thirty days. Nephrostomy was eventually removed after thirty two days. A CT scan was performed before the complete removal of nephrostomy. two month after surgery the patient is asymptomatic without hydronephrosis
